mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off equity selling over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield a negative mean 60-day forward return with a t-statistic less than -1.5 over any 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production indicate supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate profit margins and force negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 65% of observations during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings surpassing 5.0% force immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, triggering sharp short-term equity drawdowns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL crosses the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of corporate revenue expansion, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth direction fails to predict 60-day return direction with a Rank IC greater than 0.04 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid GDP expansion combined with accelerating consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary risk premium that suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDP growth and inflation shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial output alongside falling unemployment signals a synchronized economic recovery that rapidly lifts market sentiment and equity prices within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of observations when both industrial production rises and unemployment falls during early-cycle phases.
